message:
fix(render): Replace .len() with display-width in rendering contexts

Systematic audit found 11 instances where .len() (byte count) was used
instead of unicode display width for terminal rendering calculations.
This causes misaligned text with CJK characters, emoji, and other
multi-byte Unicode.

Fixes in charts.rs: y-axis width, y-label/x-label/legend rendering
Fixes in forms.rs: label width, error indicator, button centering,
  cursor positioning (+ new grapheme_display_width helper)
Fixes in traceback.rs: exception type width, height calculation
Fixes in error_boundary.rs: error message truncation
Fixes in export.rs: SVG background rect width (content.len() →
  cell.content.width())
Fixes in table.rs: saturating_add for overflow prevention, viewport
  calculation for selected row visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ unicode-width = { version = "0.2", optional = true }
 
 [features]
 canvas = ["dep:ftui-core", "dep:ftui-render", "dep:ftui-style", "dep:ftui-widgets"]
-charts = ["canvas"]
+charts = ["canvas", "dep:unicode-width"]
 clipboard = ["dep:base64", "dep:ftui-core"]
 console = ["dep:ftui-render", "dep:ftui-style", "dep:ftui-text", "dep:unicode-segmentation", "dep:unicode-width"]
 clipboard-fallback = ["clipboard"]
